svg.title: Spreadsheet-Native Orders Queue

g.text: March pilot artboard based on OrdersSheetPilotSurface plus the March 16 and March 17 rollout contracts.
Primary actions stay on-sheet. Document work remains inside Orders; accounting and shipping stay explicit owner handoffs.
Spreadsheet selections can span cells, but workflow actions stay row-scoped until one focused order remains.

svg.text: One dominant queue keeps stage, client, lines, total, and next-step cues visible so the inspector is only for deeper context.
6 visible orders, 2 drafts, 4 confirmed. Selection summary stays visible under the grid in the live runtime.
Supporting table stays compact and selection-driven instead of becoming a second full queue.
SO-1042 with 3 linked line items. Support grid follows the focused order.

g.text: 2 drafts · 4 confirmed · 6 default queue columns · queue 6 cells / 1 row
Selected SO-1042 · Draft · 1 day old · queue selection visible
Primary actions live next to selection; accounting and fulfillment stay explicit owner handoffs.
